components.title: SPARC Application
components.description: SPARC (Systematic Policy and Regulatory Compliance) is a Rails application for managing NIST 800-53 compliance documentation. This component definition covers authentication and access control implementations.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC implements account management through the User model with active, suspended, and deactivated status enumerations. Administrative users manage accounts via role assignments. The deactivate! and reactivate! methods enforce account lifecycle transitions. Account status changes are audited via AuditEvent logging. Service accounts are managed through a dedicated admin interface with full lifecycle support: create, disable, enable, transfer ownership, and soft-delete. Service account management is restricted to admin users only. Each service account has a designated owner for accountability, and ownership can be transferred between users. Automated service account lifecycle management is provided by ServiceAccountMaintenanceJob, a daily Solid Queue job that auto-disables service accounts with all-expired tokens (reason: token_expired) or exceeding the configurable SPARC_SA_INACTIVITY_DAYS inactivity threshold (default 90 days, reason: inactivity). Proactive email notifications are sent by ServiceAccountNotificationJob, a daily job that detects service accounts approaching token expiry (14-day and 7-day warnings), expired tokens, and approaching inactivity auto-disable thresholds. Notifications are delivered via ServiceAccountMailer to service account owners and all admin users, gated on SparcConfig.enable_smtp? to skip when SMTP is not configured. Instance admin credential rotation (#402/#403) generates AuditEvent rows for every lifecycle event: admin_bootstrap (first install or reset path), admin_credential_synced_from_env (boot-time reconciliation when SM rotated since last task start), admin_credential_rotated (in-task or API-initiated rotation), and admin_password_reset (manual reset via rake task).
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in app/models/user.rb, app/controllers/concerns/authorization.rb, app/controllers/admin/service_accounts_controller.rb, app/jobs/service_account_maintenance_job.rb, app/jobs/service_account_notification_job.rb, and app/mailers/service_account_mailer.rb. Tested by spec/models/user_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC enforces access control through authorize_admin!, authorize_role!, and authorize_permission! controller concerns. The authorization framework supports 57 granular permission keys mapped to resource types and actions. Unauthorized access attempts are blocked and audited. Phase 2 API controllers (control_catalogs, profile_documents, cdef_documents, control_mappings) enforce Bearer token authentication on all endpoints, with admin-only gates on catalog and mapping write operations. The API discovery endpoint (GET /api/v1/available) enforces authorization scoping — callers only see endpoints and HTTP methods they are permitted to use, preventing unauthorized endpoint enumeration. Service account API tokens support endpoint-scoped access via an allowed_endpoints JSONB attribute, restricting each token to a declared set of API paths. Requests to endpoints not in the allowlist are rejected. Service account tokens also enforce CIDR-based IP restrictions, blocking requests from IP addresses outside the configured allowlist.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in app/controllers/concerns/authorization.rb, app/models/role.rb, app/models/api_token.rb, app/controllers/api/v1/control_catalogs_controller.rb, app/controllers/api/v1/profile_documents_controller.rb, app/controllers/api/v1/cdef_documents_controller.rb, app/controllers/api/v1/control_mappings_controller.rb, app/controllers/api/v1/discovery_controller.rb, and app/controllers/admin/service_accounts_controller.rb. Tested by spec/controllers/ and spec/models/role_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC enforces separation of duties through 29 seeded roles: 10 instance-scoped and 19 boundary-scoped. Each role defines a distinct set of permissions preventing any single user from accumulating conflicting privileges. Role assignments are managed by administrators and audited.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in app/models/role.rb with role seeds in db/seeds/. Tested by spec/models/role_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC implements least privilege through granular JSONB permission structures defined in Role::PERMISSION_GROUPS. Each role specifies read/write access per resource type (SSP, SAR, CDEF, catalogs, profiles, users). The authorize_permission! method enforces these constraints on every request. Phase 2 API endpoints enforce admin-only gates for catalog and mapping write operations, while profile and CDEF endpoints are accessible to all authenticated users. The API discovery endpoint (GET /api/v1/available) omits write methods for read-only callers and hides entire admin-only endpoints (users, catalog writes, mapping writes) from non-admin users, ensuring the response reflects least-privilege scoping. Service accounts cannot be assigned the admin role, ensuring privileged functions remain restricted to human users. Service account tokens enforce least privilege through endpoint-scoped access (allowed_endpoints JSONB) and CIDR-based IP restrictions, limiting each token to only the API paths and network ranges required for its function.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in app/models/role.rb (PERMISSION_GROUPS), app/controllers/concerns/authorization.rb, and app/controllers/admin/service_accounts_controller.rb. Phase 2 controllers: app/controllers/api/v1/control_catalogs_controller.rb, app/controllers/api/v1/profile_documents_controller.rb, app/controllers/api/v1/cdef_documents_controller.rb, app/controllers/api/v1/control_mappings_controller.rb, app/controllers/api/v1/discovery_controller.rb. Tested by spec/models/role_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC handles unsuccessful login attempts through session management in the Authentication concern. Failed authentication attempts are logged as audit events with the originating IP address and user agent for monitoring and response.

SPARC displays a configurable login consent banner controlled by the SPARC_BANNER_ENABLED and SPARC_BANNER_MESSAGE environment variables. When enabled, a Bootstrap 5 modal presents the banner content (loaded from file and sanitized for XSS) that users must acknowledge before proceeding to authentication.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in app/views/sessions/ and app/models/sparc_config.rb. Tested by spec/requests/sessions_spec.rb and spec/system/login_banner_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC implements session lock via configurable idle timeout controlled by the SPARC_SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINUTES environment variable (default 60 minutes). The check_session_timeout method in the Authentication concern evaluates session freshness on every request and forces re-authentication when the idle threshold is exceeded.

SPARC provides explicit session termination through the logout action. The end_session method resets the Rails session, clears session cookies, and redirects the user to the login page. All session terminations are recorded as audit events.

SPARC controls permitted actions without identification through the SparcConfig.any_auth_enabled? gate. When no authentication providers are enabled, access is restricted to read-only operations. The configuration is enforced at the controller level before any authorization check.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in app/models/sparc_config.rb and app/controllers/concerns/authentication.rb. Tested by spec/models/sparc_config_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC enforces remote access protection through FORCE_SSL with HSTS enforcement (1 year max-age, includeSubDomains, preload) in the production environment. All remote connections are encrypted via TLS, and unencrypted HTTP requests are automatically redirected to HTTPS. Service account API tokens enforce CIDR-based IP restrictions via the cidr_allowlist attribute, limiting remote API access to approved network ranges.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in config/environments/production.rb and app/models/api_token.rb. Tested by deployment verification.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC supports multi-factor identification and authentication through multiple configurable providers: local login with bcrypt password hashing, OIDC with SPARC_OIDC_FORCE_MFA enforcement, LDAP directory authentication, and OAuth (GitHub, GitLab) federation. The Authentication concern orchestrates provider selection and credential verification. For API access, SPARC supports 3 mutually exclusive auth modes selected via the SPARC_API_AUTH env var: local (SPARC Bearer tokens only), oidc (OIDC JWT only), and hybrid (OIDC JWT for human users, SPARC Bearer tokens restricted to service accounts with the service_account flag). SessionsController blocks service account web login. Boot-time validation in config/initializers/api_auth.rb ensures correct configuration at startup.
implemented-requirements.remarks: CONDITIONAL COVERAGE: Full IA-2 (including MFA enhancements IA-2(1) and IA-2(2)) requires SPARC_ENABLE_OIDC=true and SPARC_OIDC_FORCE_MFA=true, delegating MFA to the OIDC identity provider. Local-only login (SPARC_ENABLE_LOCAL_LOGIN=true without OIDC) satisfies identification and single-factor authentication but does NOT satisfy MFA requirements. LDAP coverage depends on the directory server's MFA configuration. JWT API authentication requires SPARC_ENABLE_OIDC=true and SPARC_OIDC_ISSUER_URL to be configured. Hybrid mode enforces OIDC for humans and SPARC tokens for service accounts only. Recommended production config: enable OIDC with force MFA, disable local login. Implemented in app/controllers/concerns/authentication.rb, app/controllers/concerns/api_authentication.rb, config/initializers/api_auth.rb, and app/controllers/omniauth_callbacks_controller.rb. Tested by spec/requests/sessions_spec.rb and spec/requests/omniauth_callbacks_spec.rb. See docs/dev/issue_rules.md for the full auth mode coverage matrix.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC uniquely identifies users via email address with case-insensitive uniqueness validation enforced at both the application and database levels. Each user record has a unique database ID and email, preventing duplicate identifiers across the system. Service accounts are assigned UUID-based identifiers and their API tokens use the sparc_sa_ prefix, enabling clear identification and differentiation of service account tokens from human user tokens in logs and audit records.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in app/models/user.rb and app/models/api_token.rb with database unique index. Tested by spec/models/user_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC manages authenticator credentials through dual authentication mechanisms: bcrypt password hashing with a minimum length of 12 characters for interactive login, SHA-256 digested SPARC Bearer tokens for API access, and RS256 JWT signature verification for Okta-issued JWTs presented to API endpoints. Password expiry is enforced via the SPARC_PASSWORD_EXPIRY_DAYS configuration (default 30 days), requiring periodic credential rotation. In hybrid mode (SPARC_API_AUTH=hybrid), RS256 JWT is used for human users and SHA-256 token digest for service accounts. Boot-time validation in config/initializers/api_auth.rb ensures correct auth configuration at startup (e.g., OIDC settings present when oidc/hybrid mode selected). JWKS caching uses Rails.cache for multi-process safety. Service account tokens are stored as SHA-256 digests, use the sparc_sa_ prefix for identification, enforce mandatory expiry dates, and track owner accountability. Token regeneration creates a new SHA-256 digest and logs the event. Instance admin credential rotation (#402/#403) is automated through three coordinated mechanisms: (1) bootstrap_admin reconciles the admin password from the SPARC_ADMIN_PASSWORD env var (ECS-injected from Secrets Manager) on every container start, applying any rotation that occurred while the previous task was running; (2) the sparc:rotate_admin_credentials rake task generates a new password and atomically updates both the DB and AWS Secrets Manager via PutSecretValue; (3) POST /api/v1/admin/refresh_credentials accepts a new password from a sparc-iac-managed Lambda (authenticated via service account Bearer token with the admin.rotate_credentials permission) and bcrypts it into the DB. SPARC's task role holds write-only PutSecretValue access on admin-credentials — it cannot read the secret directly, preserving MFA-gated break-glass retrieval through the AWS Console.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Full coverage for local login (bcrypt, min 12 chars, expiry). API authenticator management covers both SHA-256 digested SPARC tokens and RS256 JWT signature verification against the OIDC provider's JWKS endpoint. JWT API authentication requires SPARC_ENABLE_OIDC=true and SPARC_OIDC_ISSUER_URL to be configured. When OIDC is enabled, password management for federated users is delegated to the OIDC IdP. LDAP password management is delegated to the directory server. Admin credential rotation (#402/#403) is gated by SPARC_ADMIN_REFRESH_ENABLED=true (off by default — fail closed) and requires sparc-iac counterpart provisioning per risk-sentinel/sparc-iac#197. Implemented in app/models/user.rb, app/models/api_token.rb, app/controllers/concerns/api_authentication.rb, app/controllers/admin/service_accounts_controller.rb, app/services/admin_credential_rotation_service.rb, app/controllers/api/v1/admin/credentials_controller.rb, lib/tasks/admin.rake, and config/initializers/api_auth.rb. Tested by spec/models/user_spec.rb, spec/models/api_token_spec.rb, spec/services/admin_credential_rotation_service_spec.rb, spec/lib/tasks/admin_rake_spec.rb, and spec/requests/api/v1/admin/credentials_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC supports non-organizational user identification and authentication through OIDC federation with external identity providers including Okta, Microsoft Entra ID, GitHub, and GitLab. The OmniAuth callbacks controller handles federated identity assertions and maps external identities to local user accounts. External users can also authenticate to API endpoints via Okta-issued JWTs, enabling non-organizational user identification for programmatic access without requiring a local SPARC account with a pre-provisioned API token.
implemented-requirements.remarks: CONDITIONAL COVERAGE: Requires SPARC_ENABLE_OIDC=true or OAuth providers enabled. Not met with local-only login (no federation capability). JWT API authentication for external users requires SPARC_ENABLE_OIDC=true and SPARC_OIDC_ISSUER_URL to be configured. Implemented in app/controllers/omniauth_callbacks_controller.rb, app/controllers/concerns/api_authentication.rb, and app/models/sparc_config.rb. Tested by spec/requests/omniauth_callbacks_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC enforces re-authentication by terminating idle sessions after a configurable timeout period (SPARC_SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINUTES). When the idle threshold is exceeded, the user is redirected to the login page and must re-authenticate to regain access. The timeout is checked on every authenticated request.

SPARC delegates identity proofing to configured OIDC identity providers, which perform identity verification according to their own policies before issuing identity assertions. The application consumes verified identity claims from the provider's token response.
implemented-requirements.remarks: CONDITIONAL COVERAGE: Requires SPARC_ENABLE_OIDC=true. Not met with local-only login (self-registration with no identity proofing). Implemented in app/controllers/omniauth_callbacks_controller.rb. Identity proofing is handled by the external OIDC provider.
